report_name: Germany Accuses Russia of Orchestrating Large-Scale Signal Phishing Attack on Politicians

threat_actors.name: Russian state-sponsored actor
threat_actors.description: A Russian state-sponsored threat actor accused by the German government of conducting a large-scale, sophisticated phishing campaign against the Signal messenger accounts of German politicians and officials for espionage purposes.
threat_actors.goals: To compromise Signal accounts for accessing sensitive communications, contacts, and group memberships, and potentially to impersonate victims for intelligence gathering or disinformation.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A widespread phishing campaign was conducted via the Signal messenger platform to trick high-profile German individuals into actions that would lead to account takeover.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used the Signal messenger service to send phishing messages that masqueraded as official communications from the Signal support team, leveraging the platform's trusted status.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The phishing messages likely tricked users into clicking a malicious link as one of the potential methods to facilitate an account takeover.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of the campaign was to hijack victims' Signal accounts by socially engineering them into providing a phone number and registration PIN or verification code, allowing the attacker to register the account on their own device.

identities.name: German Parliament
identities.description: The German Parliament (Bundestag) was a primary target of the Signal phishing campaign, with members' accounts being compromised.

identities.name: German government officials
identities.description: A class of victims targeted in the Signal phishing campaign, including civil servants and diplomats.

identities.name: German journalists
identities.description: Journalists in Germany were among the high-profile individuals targeted by the Signal phishing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Device for Account Takeover
infrastructure.description: A device controlled by the attackers, used to re-register and take over a victim's Signal account after obtaining their phone number and a verification code.
